details: HTTP 5xx error rate: min=0.0, avg=0.79886, max=2.91942 errors/s (peak at 15:59:09 UTC) *** SPIKE (3.7×avg) ***
`up` metric: min=0.0, avg=0.746, max=1.0 (intermittent unavailability for metrics scraping)
Container memory: max=507596800 bytes (507.6MB) at 16:20:54 UTC (50% higher than avg=228.8MB)
Container CPU: max=0.0264 cores at 16:15:24 UTC *** SPIKE (4.0×avg) ***
DNS lookup timeouts for `front-end`: 2236 errors (e.g., `dial tcp: lookup front-end on 127.0.0.11:53: read udp 127.0.0.1:59534->127.0.0.11:53: i/o timeout` at 16:24:07 UTC ×45)

root_cause.summary: The `front-end` service experienced a memory leak and resource exhaustion, leading to intermittent unavailability, 5xx errors, and cascading DNS failures in the `edge-router`.

causal_chain.event: Service starts with unstable metrics endpoint (`up` metric fluctuates between 0 and 1).
HTTP 5xx error rate spikes to 2.91942 errors/s (3.7×avg), indicating internal failures.
causal_chain.evidence: `job=frontend, status_code=500: min=0.0, avg=0.79886, max=2.91942 (peak at 15:59:09 UTC)`

causal_chain.event: HTTP response latency peaks at 0.01259s, suggesting degraded performance.
Container CPU usage spikes to 0.0264 cores (4.0×avg), indicating resource exhaustion.
Container memory usage peaks at 507.6MB (50% higher than avg=228.8MB), confirming a memory leak.
DNS lookup timeouts for `front-end` begin, as the service becomes intermittently unreachable.
causal_chain.evidence: `[ERROR ×45] time="2026-05-07T16:24:07Z" level=warning msg="Error forwarding to http://front-end:8079, err: dial tcp: lookup front-end on 127.0.0.11:53: read udp 127.0.0.1:59534->127.0.0.11:53: i/o timeout"`

contributing_factors: Insufficient CPU/memory limits for `front-end`, leading to throttling and crashes.
Lack of circuit breakers or retry policies in `edge-router`, amplifying DNS failures.
No auto-scaling for `front-end`, preventing recovery from resource exhaustion.
Instrumentation gaps in `front-end` traces, obscuring visibility into request failures.

evidence.description: `job=frontend, status_code=500: min=0.0, avg=0.79886, max=2.91942 (peak at 15:59:09 UTC)` (HTTP 5xx errors)
`__name__=up, instance=front-end:8079, job=frontend: min=0.0, avg=0.746, max=1.0` (intermittent unavailability)
`container_label_com_docker_compose_service=front-end: min=61591552.0, avg=228840218.74627, max=507596800.0 (peak at 16:20:54 UTC)` (memory leak)

`[ERROR ×45] time="2026-05-07T16:24:07Z" level=warning msg="Error forwarding to http://front-end:8079, err: dial tcp: lookup front-end on 127.0.0.11:53: read udp 127.0.0.1:59534->127.0.0.11:53: i/o timeout"` (DNS failures in `edge-router`)
`container_label_com_docker_compose_service=front-end: min=0.00018, avg=0.00665, max=0.0264 (peak at 16:15:24 UTC)` (CPU spike)

impact:
End-users experienced failed or degraded requests when accessing the `front-end` service, including:
- HTTP 500 errors (3.7× spike at 15:59:09 UTC).
- Intermittent unavailability due to memory leaks and CPU throttling.
- DNS resolution failures in `edge-router`, preventing routing to `front-end`.

Downstream services (`carts`, `orders`, `shipping`) also experienced CPU throttling, likely due to retries or cascading failures from `front-end` instability.
